verses.text: Moses fears they will not believe; God turns his rod to a serpent and back, a sign. Rod sign. God equips His servant with proof.
Moses fears they will not believe; God turns his rod to a serpent and back, a sign. Rod sign. God equips His servant with proof.
Moses fears they will not believe; God turns his rod to a serpent and back, a sign. Rod sign. God equips His servant with proof.

Moses fears they will not believe; God turns his rod to a serpent and back, a sign. Rod sign. God equips His servant with proof.
His hand turns leprous and is healed; if unbelieved, river-water shall become blood. More signs. God multiplies confirmations for the doubting.
His hand turns leprous and is healed; if unbelieved, river-water shall become blood. More signs. God multiplies confirmations for the doubting.
His hand turns leprous and is healed; if unbelieved, river-water shall become blood. More signs. God multiplies confirmations for the doubting.
His hand turns leprous and is healed; if unbelieved, river-water shall become blood. More signs. God multiplies confirmations for the doubting.
Moses pleads he is 'slow of speech'; God says, 'I will be with thy mouth, and teach thee.' Maker of mouths. He who made the tongue supplies the words.
Moses pleads he is 'slow of speech'; God says, 'I will be with thy mouth, and teach thee.' Maker of mouths. He who made the tongue supplies the words.
Moses pleads he is 'slow of speech'; God says, 'I will be with thy mouth, and teach thee.' Maker of mouths. He who made the tongue supplies the words.
Moses still begs God send another; angered, God appoints Aaron to be his spokesman. Reluctant. God provides Aaron, but the hesitation displeased Him.

Moses still begs God send another; angered, God appoints Aaron to be his spokesman. Reluctant. God provides Aaron, but the hesitation displeased Him.
Moses takes the rod and returns to Egypt with his family and his father-in-law's blessing. Sets out. He goes at last in obedience.

God forewarns He will 'harden Pharaoh's heart'; Israel is 'my son, even my firstborn.' God's son. Refusing to free him will cost Egypt her firstborn.

At an inn God seeks to kill Moses; Zipporah circumcises their son, and he is spared. Neglected sign. Even the deliverer must keep God's covenant.

Aaron meets him; they gather the elders, do the signs, and the people believe and worship. Believed. They bow when they hear God has visited them.
